summary: Run weak-pi pulsed ODMR on image231924_c01 to refine the resonance before Ramsey/T2star.
scientific_rationale: The completed strong-pi pODMR retry showed a clear raw/readout-aware resonance near 3.879 GHz with about 23-25% normalized dip and healthy tracking counts. Strong-pi pODMR passes the alignment screen but is not a precision mw_freq calibration, so weak-pi pODMR is the required next step before Ramsey/T2star.
comparison_targets: NV_RESEARCH_MEMORY setup reference: ms=+1 transition expected near 3.875 GHz and setup contrast about 20%
human advice: once one aligned NV is found, focus on that NV and keep initial scans around 20-30 points
expected_evidence: terminal weak-pi pODMR bridge result with savedexperiment path
raw/readout-aware weak-pi resonance review and transparent center estimate
if no usable weak-pi line appears, bridge-free review of whether to adjust weak-pi window/conditions or move candidate strategy
success_criteria: bridge queue idle before submission and pre-enqueue advisory has no hard blockers
acquisition completes 25 points over a 40 MHz window centered on the strong-pi rough center
raw/readout-aware review supports a weak-pi resonance center usable for Ramsey/T2star planning
stop_conditions: bridge queue is not idle immediately before submission
project lifecycle is canceled or do_not_submit_or_mutate_bridge_queue is true
verifier or pre-enqueue advisory reports hard blockers
auto-align counts collapse or bridge execution fails before acquiring data
bridge_action: execute pulsed_odmr_rabimodulated_v1 weak-pi pODMR via managed direct/batch path

target.seed_source: terminal strong-pi pODMR saved scan position 1DExp-seq-Rabimodulated-vary-mw_freq-2026-05-12-000634.mat

planned_measurement.analysis_plan: raw export and line-shape review first; no automatic bridge fit
